description: Introduce release-please in manifest mode to manage versioning, changelogs, and GitHub Releases for the monorepo. Starts with a single platform component covering monorepo/, with conventional commits enforced by commitlint.

userStories.title: Add release-please manifest config
userStories.description:
As a developer, I want release-please manifest configuration files at the repo root so that versioning and changelog generation are automated.

Create two files at the repo root:

1. `release-please-config.json` with:
   - `$schema` pointing to the release-please config schema
   - A single `packages` entry for `monorepo` with `release-type: simple`, `component: platform`, `changelog-path: CHANGELOG.md`, `bump-minor-pre-major: true`, `bump-patch-for-minor-pre-major: true`
   - `separate-pull-requests: false`
   - `sequential-calls: false`

2. `.release-please-manifest.json` with:
   - `{ "monorepo": "0.1.0" }`
acceptanceCriteria: release-please-config.json exists at repo root with valid JSON matching the release-please config schema
.release-please-manifest.json exists at repo root with initial version 0.1.0 for monorepo
Config uses release-type 'simple' and component name 'platform'
separate-pull-requests is false
Both files are valid JSON (parseable without errors)

userStories.title: Add release-please GitHub Action
userStories.description:
As a developer, I want a GitHub Actions workflow that runs release-please on every push to main so that Release PRs are created and GitHub Releases are published automatically.

Create `.github/workflows/release.yml` that:
- Triggers on push to main branch
- Has contents: write and pull-requests: write permissions
- Uses googleapis/release-please-action@v4
- References the manifest and config files from US-001
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/release.yml exists and is valid YAML
Workflow triggers on push to main branch only
Workflow has contents: write and pull-requests: write permissions
Uses googleapis/release-please-action@v4 with manifest-file and config-file inputs
Outputs releases_created and paths_released for downstream jobs to consume

userStories.title: Add commitlint to enforce conventional commits
userStories.description:
As a developer, I want CI to validate that PR titles follow the Conventional Commits spec so that non-conventional commits cannot reach main and break release-please changelog generation.

Add a GitHub Actions workflow that:
- Runs on pull_request events
- Validates the PR title against @commitlint/config-conventional (or equivalent lightweight check)
- Fails the check if the title doesn't conform

Note: Since we use squash-merge, validating the PR title is sufficient (it becomes the commit message on main). Avoid heavy Node.js dependencies if possible — a simple regex-based check or a lightweight action is preferred.
acceptanceCriteria: A CI check runs on pull_request events that validates the PR title
PR titles following conventional commits spec (feat:, fix:, chore:, etc.) pass the check
PR titles not following the spec fail the check with a clear error message
Breaking change indicators (feat!:, fix!:, BREAKING CHANGE footer) are accepted
Scoped commits like feat(feed-reader): are accepted
The check does not require installing Node.js or heavy dependencies if avoidable

userStories.title: Document release workflow in ARCHITECTURE.md
userStories.description:
As a developer, I want the release management strategy documented in ARCHITECTURE.md so that all contributors understand the versioning, tagging, and release process.

Add a 'Release Management' section to ARCHITECTURE.md covering:
- Conventional commit requirements and examples
- How release-please works (Release PRs, auto-tagging, GitHub Releases)
- Tag naming convention (component-vX.Y.Z)
- How to add a new component to release-please
- How Argo CD references component tags for deployments

Also update the Architecture Diagram section to reference D2 instead of Mermaid C4 (it still says Mermaid).
acceptanceCriteria: ARCHITECTURE.md contains a 'Release Management' section
Section documents conventional commit format with examples
Section explains release-please manifest mode and the Release PR workflow
Section lists the tag naming convention (platform-vX.Y.Z, service-vX.Y.Z, etc.)
Section explains how to register a new component
Section explains how Argo CD consumes tags
Architecture Diagram section updated to reference D2 instead of Mermaid C4

userStories.title: Validate release setup with a dry-run release
userStories.description:
As a developer, I want to verify that release-please correctly opens a Release PR after a conventional commit is pushed to main.

This is a validation step: after the previous stories are merged to main, push a conventional commit and verify that:
1. The release-please action runs
2. A Release PR is opened with correct version bump (0.1.0 → 0.2.0 for a feat, or 0.1.1 for a fix)
3. The PR updates CHANGELOG.md in monorepo/
4. The PR updates .release-please-manifest.json
5. The tag prefix is 'platform-v'
acceptanceCriteria: release-please GitHub Action runs successfully on push to main
A Release PR is opened automatically by release-please
The Release PR contains a CHANGELOG.md update in monorepo/
The Release PR updates .release-please-manifest.json with the new version
The proposed tag uses the platform-v prefix
userStories.notes: This task requires merging to main and observing release-please behavior. Human review needed to verify the Release PR looks correct.
